```text
     1→package droidcli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"

     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"strings"

    12→	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"

    14→)
    15→
    16→type watchState struct {
    17→	lastProcessed map[string]int64
    18→}
    19→
    20→func watchSessions(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    21→	if sessionCallback == nil {
    22→		return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
    23→	}
    24→
    25→	sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
    26→	if err != nil {
    27→		return err
    28→	}
    29→
    30→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    31→	if err != nil {
    32→		return fmt.Errorf("droidcli: failed to create watcher: %w", err)
    33→	}
    34→	defer func() {
    35→		_ = watcher.Close()
    36→	}()
    37→
    38→	state := &watchState{lastProcessed: make(map[string]int64)}
    39→
    40→	// Initial scan to process existing sessions
    41→	if err := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); err != nil {
    42→		slog.Debug("droidcli: initial scan failed", "error", err)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	// Setup directory watching (handles non-existent dirs)
    46→	if err := setupDirectoryWatches(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
    47→		slog.Debug("droidcli: setup watches failed", "error", err)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	// Event loop
    51→	for {
    52→		select {
    53→		case <-ctx.Done():
    54→			return ctx.Err()
    55→		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
    56→			if !ok {
    57→				return nil
    58→			}
    59→			handleWatchEvent(event, watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
    60→		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
    61→			if !ok {
    62→				return nil
    63→			}
    64→			slog.Debug("droidcli: watcher error", "error", err)
    65→		}
    66→	}
    67→}
    68→
    69→// setupDirectoryWatches adds watches to the sessions directory and all subdirectories.
    70→// If the sessions directory doesn't exist, it watches parent directories so it can
    71→// detect when the sessions directory is created.
    72→func setupDirectoryWatches(watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string) error {
    73→	// Check if sessions directory exists
    74→	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
    75→		// Watch parent (.factory) for sessions dir creation
    76→		parentDir := filepath.Dir(sessionsDir)
    77→		if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
    78→			// Watch home for .factory creation
    79→			homeDir := filepath.Dir(parentDir)
    80→			slog.Debug("droidcli: watching home for .factory creation", "path", homeDir)
    81→			return watcher.Add(homeDir)
    82→		}
    83→		slog.Debug("droidcli: watching .factory for sessions creation", "path", parentDir)
    84→		return watcher.Add(parentDir)
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// Watch sessions dir and all subdirectories
    88→	return filepath.WalkDir(sessionsDir, func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, err error) error {
    89→		if err != nil {
    90→			return nil
    91→		}
    92→		if d.IsDir() {
    93→			if watchErr := watcher.Add(path); watchErr != nil {
    94→				slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to watch directory", "path", path, "error", watchErr)
    95→			} else {
    96→				slog.Debug("droidcli: watching directory", "path", path)
    97→			}
    98→		}
    99→		return nil
   100→	})
   101→}
   102→
   103→// handleWatchEvent processes fsnotify events, adding watches to new directories
   104→// and processing changed JSONL files.
   105→func handleWatchEvent(event fsnotify.Event, watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
   106→	// Handle directory creation - add new watch
   107→	if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   108→		info, err := os.Stat(event.Name)
   109→		if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   110→			if watchErr := watcher.Add(event.Name); watchErr == nil {
   111→				slog.Debug("droidcli: watching new directory", "path", event.Name)
   112→			}
   113→
   114→			// Check if this is the sessions directory being created
   115→			if event.Name == sessionsDir {
   116→				if err := setupDirectoryWatches(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
   117→					slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to setup watches for new sessions dir", "error", err)
   118→				}
   119→			}
   120→			return
   121→		}
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	// Only process .jsonl files
   125→	if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".jsonl") {
   126→		return
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	// Only process Create and Write events
   130→	if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
   131→		return
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	slog.Debug("droidcli: jsonl file event", "op", event.Op.String(), "file", event.Name)
   135→
   136→	// Process the specific file that changed
   137→	processSessionFile(event.Name, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
   138→}
   139→
   140→// processSessionFile processes a single JSONL file if it has been modified.
   141→func processSessionFile(filePath string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
   142→	info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		return
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	modTime := info.ModTime().UnixNano()
   148→	if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[filePath]; ok && last >= modTime {
   149→		return
   150→	}
   151→
   152→	if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(filePath, projectPath) {
   153→		return
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	session, err := parseFactorySession(filePath)
   157→	if err != nil {
   158→		slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session", "path", filePath, "error", err)
   159→		return
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
   163→	if chat == nil {
   164→		return
   165→	}
   166→
   167→	state.lastProcessed[filePath] = modTime
   168→	dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
   169→}
   170→
   171→// scanAndProcessSessions scans all session files and processes any that have been modified.
   172→func scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) error {
   173→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
   174→	if err != nil {
   175→		return err
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	for _, file := range files {
   179→		if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[file.Path]; ok && last >= file.ModTime {
   180→			continue
   181→		}
   182→		if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
   183→			continue
   184→		}
   185→		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
   186→		if err != nil {
   187→			slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
   188→			continue
   189→		}
   190→		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
   191→		if chat == nil {
   192→			continue
   193→		}
   194→		state.lastProcessed[file.Path] = file.ModTime
   195→		dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
   196→	}
   197→	return nil
   198→}
   199→
   200→func dispatchSession(sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   201→	if sessionCallback == nil || session == nil {
   202→		return
   203→	}
   204→	go func() {
   205→		defer func() {
   206→			if r := recover(); r != nil {
   207→				slog.Error("droidcli: session callback panicked", "panic", r)
   208→			}
   209→		}()
   210→		sessionCallback(session)
   211→	}()
   212→}
   213→
```

 `./pkg/providers/droidcli/path_utils.go`

```text
     1→package droidcli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"io/fs"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→	"sort"
     9→)
    10→
    11→const (
    12→	factoryRootDir     = ".factory"
    13→	factorySessionsDir = "sessions"
    14→)
    15→
    16→type sessionFile struct {
    17→	Path     string
    18→	ModTime  int64
    19→	FileName string
    20→}
    21→
    22→func resolveSessionsDir() (string, error) {
    23→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
    24→	if err != nil {
    25→		return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve home dir: %w", err)
    26→	}
    27→	dir := filepath.Join(home, factoryRootDir, factorySessionsDir)
    28→	return dir, nil
    29→}
    30→
    31→func listSessionFiles() ([]sessionFile, error) {
    32→	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
    33→	if err != nil {
    34→		return nil, err
    35→	}
    36→	files := make([]sessionFile, 0, 64)
    37→	walker := func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, walkErr error) error {
    38→		if walkErr != nil {
    39→			return nil
    40→		}
    41→		if d.IsDir() {
    42→			return nil
    43→		}
    44→		if filepath.Ext(d.Name()) != ".jsonl" {
    45→			return nil
    46→		}
    47→		info, err := d.Info()
    48→		if err != nil {
    49→			return nil
    50→		}
    51→		files = append(files, sessionFile{
    52→			Path:     path,
    53→			ModTime:  info.ModTime().UnixNano(),
    54→			FileName: d.Name(),
    55→		})
    56→		return nil
    57→	}
    58→	if err := filepath.WalkDir(dir, walker); err != nil {
    59→		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
    60→			return []sessionFile{}, nil
    61→		}
    62→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("droidcli: unable to read sessions dir: %w", err)
    63→	}
    64→	sort.Slice(files, func(i, j int) bool {
    65→		return files[i].ModTime > files[j].ModTime
    66→	})
    67→	return files, nil
    68→}
    69→
    70→func findSessionFileByID(sessionID string) (string, error) {
    71→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
    72→	if err != nil {
    73→		return "", err
    74→	}
    75→	for _, file := range files {
    76→		candidate := file.FileName
    77→		if candidate == sessionID || trimExt(candidate) == sessionID {
    78→			return file.Path, nil
    79→		}
    80→	}
    81→	return "", nil
    82→}
    83→
    84→func trimExt(name string) string {
    85→	ext := filepath.Ext(name)
    86→	if ext == "" {
    87→		return name
    88→	}
    89→	return name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
    90→}
    91→
    92→// settingsPathFromSession returns the path to the .settings.json file
    93→// that corresponds to a session JSONL file.
    94→// Example: /path/to/session.jsonl -> /path/to/session.settings.json
    95→func settingsPathFromSession(sessionPath string) string {
    96→	dir := filepath.Dir(sessionPath)
    97→	base := filepath.Base(sessionPath)
    98→	ext := filepath.Ext(base)
    99→	nameWithoutExt := base[:len(base)-len(ext)]
   100→	return filepath.Join(dir, nameWithoutExt+".settings.json")
   101→}
   102→
```

```text
   328→
   329→func sessionMentionsProject(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
   330→	projectPath = strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
   331→	if projectPath == "" {
   332→		return false
   333→	}
   334→
   335→	canonicalProject := canonicalizePath(projectPath)
   336→	if sessionRoot := extractSessionWorkspaceRoot(filePath); sessionRoot != "" {
   337→		canonicalRoot := canonicalizePath(sessionRoot)
   338→		if canonicalProject != "" && canonicalRoot != "" {
   339→			return canonicalRoot == canonicalProject
   340→		}
   341→		return strings.TrimSpace(sessionRoot) == projectPath
   342→	}
   343→
   344→	return sessionMentionsProjectText(filePath, projectPath)
   345→}
   346→
   347→func extractSessionWorkspaceRoot(filePath string) string {
   348→	file, err := os.Open(filePath)
   349→	if err != nil {
   350→		return ""
   351→	}
   352→	defer func() {
   353→		_ = file.Close()
   354→	}()
   355→
   356→	var workspaceRoot string
   357→	scanErr := scanLines(file, func(_ int, line string) error {
   358→		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
   359→		if trimmed == "" {
   360→			return nil
   361→		}
   362→		var env jsonlEnvelope
   363→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &env); err != nil {
   364→			return nil
   365→		}
   366→		if env.Type != "session_start" {
   367→			return nil
   368→		}
   369→		var event sessionStartEvent
   370→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &event); err != nil {
   371→			return nil
   372→		}
   373→		workspaceRoot = firstNonEmpty(event.CWD, event.WorkspaceRoot, event.Session.CWD, event.Session.WorkspaceRoot)
   374→		return errStopScan
   375→	})
   376→	if scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, errStopScan) {
   377→		return ""
   378→	}
   379→
   380→	return strings.TrimSpace(workspaceRoot)
   381→}
   382→
   383→func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
   384→	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(path)
   385→	if trimmed == "" {
   386→		return ""
   387→	}
   388→	canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(trimmed)
   389→	if err == nil {
   390→		return canonical
   391→	}
   392→	if abs, absErr := filepath.Abs(trimmed); absErr == nil {
   393→		return filepath.Clean(abs)
   394→	}
   395→	return filepath.Clean(trimmed)
   396→}
```

```text
     1→package spi
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"log/slog"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"path/filepath"
     9→	"regexp"
    10→	"strings"
    11→	"unicode"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
    14→	"golang.org/x/text/runes"
    15→	"golang.org/x/text/transform"
    16→	"golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// xmlTagsRe matches XML-style tag blocks including their content, such as
    20→// <ide_opened_file>...</ide_opened_file> or self-closing <tag />.
    21→// These are injected by IDE tools/system prompts and should be stripped before
    22→// processing user message text for filename or display purposes.
    23→//
    24→// The regex has two alternations:
    25→//   - <tag ...>content</tag>  — matched with (?s) so . spans newlines; uses .*? (lazy)
    26→//     to consume the shortest content between matching open/close tags.
    27→//   - <tag ... />             — self-closing tags.
    28→//
    29→// Known limitation: nested tags with the same name (e.g. <t><t>x</t></t>) will
    30→// only match up to the first closing tag, leaving a dangling </t>. This is
    31→// acceptable because IDE-injected tags are not nested this way.
    32→var xmlTagsRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?s)<[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?>.*?</[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*>|<[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*(?:\s[^>]*)?/>`)
    33→
    34→// whitespaceRe collapses any run of Unicode whitespace into a single space.
    35→var whitespaceRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
    36→
    37→// stripXMLTags removes XML-style tag blocks (and their content) from s and
    38→// returns the trimmed result with normalized whitespace. This cleans up injected
    39→// IDE/system context that precedes the real user message text while preserving
    40→// word boundaries where tags appeared between tokens.
    41→func stripXMLTags(s string) string {
    42→	cleaned := xmlTagsRe.ReplaceAllString(s, " ")
    43→	cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
    44→	cleaned = whitespaceRe.ReplaceAllString(cleaned, " ")
    45→	return cleaned
    46→}
    47→
    48→// extractWordsFromMessage extracts up to maxWords from the message, handling various edge cases
    49→func extractWordsFromMessage(message string, maxWords int) []string {
    50→	if message == "" {
    51→		return []string{}
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	// Normalize unicode and remove accents
    55→	t := transform.Chain(norm.NFD, runes.Remove(runes.In(unicode.Mn)), norm.NFC)
    56→	normalized, _, _ := transform.String(t, message)
    57→
    58→	// Convert to lowercase
    59→	normalized = strings.ToLower(normalized)
    60→
    61→	// Replace special characters with their word equivalents
    62→	normalized = strings.ReplaceAll(normalized, "@", " at ")
    63→	normalized = strings.ReplaceAll(normalized, "&", " and ")
    64→	normalized = strings.ReplaceAll(normalized, "#", " hash ")
    65→
    66→	// Remove all punctuation and special characters, replace with spaces
    67→	// This regex keeps letters, numbers, and spaces
    68→	re := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-z0-9\s]+`)
    69→	normalized = re.ReplaceAllString(normalized, " ")
    70→
    71→	// Split into words and filter out empty strings
    72→	words := strings.Fields(normalized)
    73→
    74→	// Take up to maxWords
    75→	if len(words) > maxWords {
    76→		words = words[:maxWords]
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	return words
    80→}
    81→
    82→// generateFilenameFromWords creates a filename from words, handling edge cases.
    83→// Enforces lowercase to guarantee filesystem-safe, consistent filenames regardless
    84→// of how the words were prepared by the caller.
    85→func generateFilenameFromWords(words []string) string {
    86→	if len(words) == 0 {
    87→		return ""
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	// Join words with hyphens and enforce lowercase as a safety guarantee
    91→	filename := strings.ToLower(strings.Join(words, "-"))
    92→
    93→	// Ensure no double hyphens
    94→	for strings.Contains(filename, "--") {
    95→		filename = strings.ReplaceAll(filename, "--", "-")
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	// Remove leading/trailing hyphens
    99→	filename = strings.Trim(filename, "-")
   100→
   101→	return filename
   102→}
   103→
   104→// GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage generates a filename based on the first user message content
   105→func GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(message string) string {
   106→	words := extractWordsFromMessage(stripXMLTags(message), 4)
   107→	return generateFilenameFromWords(words)
   108→}
   109→
   110→// GenerateReadableName creates a human-readable session name from a user message
   111→// The name is truncated to 100 characters at a word boundary and cleaned of excess whitespace
   112→func GenerateReadableName(message string) string {
   113→	if message == "" {
   114→		return ""
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	message = stripXMLTags(message)
   118→	if message == "" {
   119→		return ""
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// Normalize whitespace: replace newlines and multiple spaces with single space
   123→	name := strings.Join(strings.Fields(message), " ")
   124→
   125→	// Truncate to reasonable length (100 chars) at word boundary
   126→	maxLength := 100
   127→	if len(name) <= maxLength {
   128→		return name
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	// Find last space before maxLength to avoid breaking words
   132→	truncated := name[:maxLength]
   133→	lastSpace := strings.LastIndex(truncated, " ")
   134→	if lastSpace > 0 {
   135→		truncated = truncated[:lastSpace]
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	// Add ellipsis to indicate truncation
   139→	return truncated + "..."
   140→}
   141→
   142→// appendRemainingParts appends the remaining path components from parts[startIdx:] to the
   143→// result path, skipping any empty components. This is used when we can't canonicalize the
   144→// rest of the path (e.g., directory doesn't exist or can't be read).
   145→func appendRemainingParts(result string, parts []string, startIdx int) string {
   146→	for j := startIdx; j < len(parts); j++ {
   147→		if parts[j] != "" {
   148→			result = filepath.Join(result, parts[j])
   149→		}
   150→	}
   151→	return result
   152→}
   153→
   154→// GetCanonicalPath resolves a path to its canonical form by resolving symlinks
   155→// and correcting case. This is important on case-insensitive filesystems like macOS
   156→// where "/Users/Foo" and "/users/foo" refer to the same directory but would produce
   157→// different hash values.
   158→//
   159→// The algorithm works by walking the path component by component, starting from the root.
   160→// For each component, it opens the parent directory and reads its actual entries,
   161→// performing case-insensitive matching to find the correct casing on disk.
   162→// This ensures that the returned path matches the real filesystem casing, which is
   163→// important for consistent hashing and avoiding subtle bugs on case-insensitive filesystems.
   164→//
   165→// Note: This can be slow for long paths or directories with many entries, since it
   166→// requires reading each directory's contents.
   167→//
   168→// If a component doesn't exist on disk, the remaining path components are
   169→// appended as-is, making this function safe to use with paths that don't
   170→// fully exist yet.
   171→func GetCanonicalPath(p string) (string, error) {
   172→	// Ensure we have an absolute, clean path
   173→	p = filepath.Clean(p)
   174→	if !filepath.IsAbs(p) {
   175→		var err error
   176→		p, err = filepath.Abs(p)
   177→		if err != nil {
   178→			return "", err
   179→		}
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	// Resolve any symlinks in the path to get the real physical path
   183→	// This is critical for ensuring consistent path hashing across different
   184→	// ways of accessing the same directory (e.g., through a symlink vs directly)
   185→	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(p)
   186→	if err == nil && realPath != p {
   187→		// Successfully resolved symlinks - update p to the resolved path for case normalization
   188→		p = realPath
   189→	} else if err != nil {
   190→		// Symlink resolution failed (e.g., path doesn't exist yet),
   191→		// continue with the original path - we'll still normalize the case below
   192→		slog.Warn("GetCanonicalPath: symlink resolution failed, using original path",
   193→			"path", p, "error", err)
   194→	}
   195→
   196→	// Split into components
   197→	parts := strings.Split(p, string(os.PathSeparator))
   198→
   199→	// Start with root
   200→	result := string(os.PathSeparator)
   201→
   202→	// Skip empty first element (before leading /) that results from splitting "/foo/bar"
   203→	firstPartIndex := 1
   204→
   205→	// Build path component by component
   206→	for i := firstPartIndex; i < len(parts); i++ {
   207→		if parts[i] == "" {
   208→			continue
   209→		}
   210→
   211→		// Open current result directory to read its contents
   212→		f, err := os.Open(result)
   213→		if err != nil {
   214→			// Can't read directory - might not exist yet
   215→			// Append remaining path as-is
   216→			return appendRemainingParts(result, parts, i), nil
   217→		}
   218→
   219→		names, err := f.Readdirnames(-1)
   220→		_ = f.Close() // Best effort close, error doesn't affect result
   221→		if err != nil {
   222→			return "", err
   223→		}
   224→
   225→		// Find the correct case
   226→		found := false
   227→		for _, name := range names {
   228→			if strings.EqualFold(name, parts[i]) {
   229→				result = filepath.Join(result, name)
   230→				found = true
   231→				break
   232→			}
   233→		}
   234→
   235→		if !found {
   236→			// Component doesn't exist, append remaining as-is
   237→			result = appendRemainingParts(result, parts, i)
   238→			break
   239→		}
   240→	}
   241→
   242→	return result, nil
   243→}
   244→
   245→// debugBaseDirOverride allows overriding the base directory for debug output.
   246→// When set, debug files are written to this directory instead of the default
   247→// .specstory/debug/ location. This is set via the --debug-dir CLI flag or
   248→// the debug_dir config option.
   249→var debugBaseDirOverride string
   250→
   251→// SetDebugBaseDir sets the override base directory for debug output.
   252→// When set to a non-empty value, GetDebugDir will use this as the base
   253→// instead of the default .specstory/debug/ path.
   254→func SetDebugBaseDir(dir string) {
   255→	debugBaseDirOverride = dir
   256→}
   257→
   258→// GetDebugDir returns the debug directory path for a given session ID or UUID.
   259→//
   260→// The debug directory is used to store detailed JSON records and debugging
   261→// information for SpecStory CLI sessions. This is shared across all providers
   262→// (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor CLI) to maintain a consistent debug structure.
   263→//
   264→// When debugBaseDirOverride is set (via SetDebugBaseDir), the directory structure
   265→// is: <override>/<sessionID>/
   266→// Otherwise: .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
   267→//
   268→// Example:
   269→//
   270→//	debugDir := spi.GetDebugDir("abc123-def456")
   271→//	// Returns: ".specstory/debug/abc123-def456"
   272→func GetDebugDir(sessionID string) string {
   273→	if debugBaseDirOverride != "" {
   274→		return filepath.Join(debugBaseDirOverride, sessionID)
   275→	}
   276→	return filepath.Join(".specstory", "debug", sessionID)
   277→}
   278→
   279→// WriteDebugSessionData writes the SessionData as formatted JSON to the debug directory.
   280→// This provides a standardized, provider-agnostic debug output alongside provider-specific raw data.
   281→//
   282→// The file is written to: .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/session-data.json
   283→//
   284→// Returns an error if the debug directory cannot be created or the file cannot be written.
   285→func WriteDebugSessionData(sessionID string, sessionData *schema.SessionData) error {
   286→	if sessionData == nil {
   287→		return fmt.Errorf("sessionData is nil")
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	// Get debug directory
   291→	debugDir := GetDebugDir(sessionID)
   292→
   293→	// Create debug directory if it doesn't exist
   294→	if err := os.MkdirAll(debugDir, 0755); err != nil {
   295→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create debug directory: %w", err)
   296→	}
   297→
   298→	// Marshal SessionData to pretty-printed JSON
   299→	jsonData, err := json.MarshalIndent(sessionData, "", "  ")
   300→	if err != nil {
   301→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal SessionData: %w", err)
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	// Write to session-data.json
   305→	filePath := filepath.Join(debugDir, "session-data.json")
   306→	if err := os.WriteFile(filePath, jsonData, 0644); err != nil {
   307→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write session-data.json: %w", err)
   308→	}
   309→
   310→	// Log absolute path for debugging
   311→	absPath, _ := filepath.Abs(filePath)
   312→	slog.Debug("Wrote debug session data", "sessionId", sessionID, "path", absPath)
   313→	return nil
   314→}
   315→
```

```text
     1→package claudecode
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"strings"
    11→	"sync"
    12→	"time"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
    15→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
    16→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    17→)
    18→
    19→var (
    20→	watcherCtx      context.Context
    21→	watcherCancel   context.CancelFunc
    22→	watcherWg       sync.WaitGroup
    23→	watcherCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession) // Callback for session updates
    24→	watcherDebugRaw bool                        // Whether to write debug raw data files
    25→	watcherMutex    sync.RWMutex                // Protects watcherCallback and watcherDebugRaw
    26→)
    27→
    28→func init() {
    29→	watcherCtx, watcherCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    30→}
    31→
    32→// SetWatcherCallback sets the callback function for session updates
    33→func SetWatcherCallback(callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) {
    34→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    35→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    36→	watcherCallback = callback
    37→	slog.Info("SetWatcherCallback: Callback set", "isNil", callback == nil)
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ClearWatcherCallback clears the callback function
    41→func ClearWatcherCallback() {
    42→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    43→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    44→	watcherCallback = nil
    45→	slog.Info("ClearWatcherCallback: Callback cleared")
    46→}
    47→
    48→// SetWatcherDebugRaw sets whether to write debug raw data files
    49→func SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw bool) {
    50→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    51→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    52→	watcherDebugRaw = debugRaw
    53→	slog.Debug("SetWatcherDebugRaw: Debug raw set", "debugRaw", debugRaw)
    54→}
    55→
    56→// getWatcherDebugRaw returns the current debug raw setting (thread-safe)
    57→func getWatcherDebugRaw() bool {
    58→	watcherMutex.RLock()
    59→	defer watcherMutex.RUnlock()
    60→	return watcherDebugRaw
    61→}
    62→
    63→// getWatcherCallback returns the current callback function (thread-safe)
    64→func getWatcherCallback() func(*spi.AgentChatSession) {
    65→	watcherMutex.RLock()
    66→	defer watcherMutex.RUnlock()
    67→	return watcherCallback
    68→}
    69→
    70→// StopWatcher gracefully stops the watcher goroutine
    71→func StopWatcher() {
    72→	slog.Info("StopWatcher: Signaling watcher to stop")
    73→	watcherCancel()
    74→	slog.Info("StopWatcher: Waiting for watcher goroutine to finish")
    75→	watcherWg.Wait()
    76→	slog.Info("StopWatcher: Watcher stopped")
    77→}
    78→
    79→// WatchForProjectDir watches for a project directory that matches the current working directory
    80→func WatchForProjectDir() error {
    81→	slog.Info("WatchForProjectDir: Determining project directory to monitor")
    82→	claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir("")
    83→	if err != nil {
    84→		slog.Error("WatchForProjectDir: Failed to get project directory", "error", err)
    85→		// Check if error is specifically about missing Claude projects directory
    86→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "claude projects directory not found") {
    87→			slog.Error("WatchForProjectDir: Claude projects directory not found while starting setup watcher")
    88→			// Start watching for Claude directory creation
    89→			return WatchForClaudeSetup()
    90→		}
    91→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get project directory: %v", err)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	slog.Info("WatchForProjectDir: Project directory found", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
    95→	return startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir)
    96→}
    97→
    98→// startProjectWatcher starts watching a specific project directory
    99→func startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir string) error {
   100→	slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Creating file watcher", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   101→
   102→	// Create a new watcher
   103→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Increment wait group before starting goroutine
   109→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   110→
   111→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   112→	go func() {
   113→		// Decrement wait group when done
   114→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   115→
   116→		// Log when goroutine starts
   117→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine started")
   118→
   119→		// Defer cleanup
   120→		defer func() {
   121→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine exiting, closing watcher")
   122→			_ = watcher.Close() // Best effort cleanup; errors here are not recoverable
   123→		}()
   124→
   125→		// Check if project directory already exists
   126→		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); err == nil {
   127→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Project directory exists", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   128→			// Claude Code triggers file system events when it starts writing, so we'll catch the session on the first write event.
   129→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Waiting for JSONL file system events")
   130→		} else {
   131→			// Project directory doesn't exist yet, watch parent directory (or other error)
   132→			parentDir := filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)
   133→			projectName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   134→			slog.Warn("startProjectWatcher: Cannot access project directory, watching parent",
   135→				"error", err,
   136→				"parentDir", parentDir,
   137→				"projectName", projectName)
   138→
   139→			// Add parent directory to watcher
   140→			if err := watcher.Add(parentDir); err != nil {
   141→				log.UserError("Error watching parent directory: %v", err)
   142→				slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to watch parent directory", "error", err)
   143→				return
   144→			}
   145→
   146→			// Wait for project directory to be created
   147→			waitingForCreation := true
   148→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now watching for events in parent directory")
   149→			for waitingForCreation {
   150→				select {
   151→				case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   152→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled while waiting for directory creation")
   153→					return
   154→				case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   155→					if !ok {
   156→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher events channel closed")
   157→						return
   158→					}
   159→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Parent directory event",
   160→						"operation", event.Op.String(),
   161→						"path", event.Name,
   162→						"lookingFor", projectName)
   163→					eventBaseName := filepath.Base(event.Name)
   164→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Event basename comparison",
   165→						"basename", eventBaseName,
   166→						"comparingWith", projectName,
   167→						"equalFold", strings.EqualFold(eventBaseName, projectName))
   168→					if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), projectName) {
   169→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Detected project directory creation",
   170→							"path", event.Name)
   171→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Initial scan disabled; waiting for JSONL events")
   172→						// Use the actual created directory name from the event
   173→						claudeProjectDir = filepath.Join(parentDir, filepath.Base(event.Name))
   174→						// Remove parent directory and continue to watch project directory
   175→						_ = watcher.Remove(parentDir) // OK if this fails; we're moving to project dir
   176→						waitingForCreation = false
   177→					}
   178→				case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   179→					if !ok {
   180→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher errors channel closed")
   181→						return
   182→					}
   183→					log.UserWarn("Watcher error while waiting for project directory: %v", err)
   184→					slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Watcher error", "error", err)
   185→				}
   186→			}
   187→		}
   188→
   189→		// Add the project directory to the watcher
   190→		// Re-stat to get the actual directory name with correct case
   191→		actualProjectDir := claudeProjectDir
   192→		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)); err == nil {
   193→			baseName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   194→			for _, entry := range entries {
   195→				if strings.EqualFold(entry.Name(), baseName) {
   196→					actualProjectDir = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir), entry.Name())
   197→					break
   198→				}
   199→			}
   200→		}
   201→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now monitoring project directory for changes",
   202→			"directory", actualProjectDir)
   203→		if err := watcher.Add(actualProjectDir); err != nil {
   204→			log.UserWarn("Error watching project directory: %v", err)
   205→			slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to monitor directory", "error", err)
   206→			return
   207→		}
   208→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Successfully monitoring project directory for future changes")
   209→
   210→		// Watch for file events
   211→		for {
   212→			select {
   213→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   214→				slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   215→				return
   216→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   217→				if !ok {
   218→					return
   219→				}
   220→
   221→				// Only process JSONL files
   222→				if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".jsonl") {
   223→					continue
   224→				}
   225→
   226→				if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
   227→					continue
   228→				}
   229→
   230→				// Handle different event types
   231→				switch {
   232→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Create):
   233→					slog.Info("New JSONL file created", "file", event.Name)
   234→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   235→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Write):
   236→					slog.Info("JSONL file modified", "file", event.Name)
   237→					slog.Info("Triggering scan due to file modification")
   238→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   239→				}
   240→
   241→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   242→				if !ok {
   243→					return
   244→				}
   245→				log.UserError("Watcher error: %v", err)
   246→			}
   247→		}
   248→	}()
   249→
   250→	return nil
   251→}
   252→
   253→// scanJSONLFiles scans JSONL files and optionally filters processing to a specific changed file
   254→func scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir string, changedFile ...string) {
   255→	// Ensure logs are flushed even if we panic
   256→	defer func() {
   257→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
   258→			log.UserWarn("PANIC in ScanJSONLFiles: %v", r)
   259→			slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: PANIC recovered", "panic", r)
   260→		}
   261→	}()
   262→
   263→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === START SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   264→	var targetFile string
   265→	if len(changedFile) > 0 && changedFile[0] != "" {
   266→		targetFile = changedFile[0]
   267→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files with changed file",
   268→			"directory", claudeProjectDir,
   269→			"changedFile", targetFile)
   270→	} else {
   271→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   272→	}
   273→	// Parse files and create history files:
   274→	parser := NewJSONLParser()
   275→	var targetSessionUuid string
   276→
   277→	// Set the target file for conditional debug output
   278→	if targetFile != "" {
   279→		SetDebugTargetFile(targetFile)
   280→		defer ClearDebugTargetFile()
   281→
   282→		sessionID, err := extractSessionIDFromFile(targetFile)
   283→		if err != nil {
   284→			slog.Warn("ScanJSONLFiles: Failed to extract session ID from file",
   285→				"file", targetFile,
   286→				"error", err)
   287→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan due to file read error", "file", targetFile)
   288→			return
   289→		}
   290→		if sessionID == "" {
   291→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan; session ID not present yet", "file", targetFile)
   292→			return
   293→		}
   294→		targetSessionUuid = sessionID
   295→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Extracted session ID from changed file",
   296→			"sessionUuid", targetSessionUuid,
   297→			"file", targetFile)
   298→	}
   299→
   300→	var parseErr error
   301→	if targetSessionUuid != "" {
   302→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessionsForSession(claudeProjectDir, true, targetSessionUuid)
   303→	} else {
   304→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessions(claudeProjectDir, true)
   305→	}
   306→	if parseErr != nil {
   307→		log.UserWarn("Error parsing project sessions: %v", parseErr)
   308→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Error parsing project sessions", "error", parseErr)
   309→		return
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Process JSONL sessions (isAutosave = true, silent = false for autosave)
   313→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions",
   314→		"sessionCount", len(parser.Sessions),
   315→		"targetSessionUuid", targetSessionUuid)
   316→
   317→	// Get the session callback
   318→	callback := getWatcherCallback()
   319→	if callback == nil {
   320→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: No callback provided - this should not happen")
   321→		return
   322→	}
   323→
   324→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions with callback")
   325→	// Process sessions and call the callback for each
   326→	for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
   327→		// Skip empty sessions
   328→		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   329→			continue
   330→		}
   331→
   332→		// Skip if we're targeting a specific session and this isn't it
   333→		if targetSessionUuid != "" && session.SessionUuid != targetSessionUuid {
   334→			continue
   335→		}
   336→
   337→		// Convert to AgentChatSession (workspaceRoot extracted from records' cwd field)
   338→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, "", getWatcherDebugRaw())
   339→		if agentSession != nil {
   340→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Calling callback for session", "sessionId", agentSession.SessionID)
   341→			// Call the callback in a goroutine to avoid blocking
   342→			go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   343→				defer func() {
   344→					if r := recover(); r != nil {
   345→						slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Callback panicked", "panic", r)
   346→					}
   347→				}()
   348→				callback(s)
   349→			}(agentSession)
   350→		}
   351→	}
   352→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing complete")
   353→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === END SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   354→}
   355→
   356→// WatchForClaudeSetup watches for the creation of Claude directories
   357→func WatchForClaudeSetup() error {
   358→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Starting Claude directory setup watcher")
   359→	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	claudeDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude")
   365→	projectsDir := filepath.Join(claudeDir, "projects")
   366→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Directories",
   367→		"homeDir", homeDir,
   368→		"claudeDir", claudeDir,
   369→		"projectsDir", projectsDir)
   370→
   371→	// Determine what to watch
   372→	var watchDir string
   373→	var watchingFor string
   374→
   375→	if _, err := os.Stat(claudeDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   376→		// .claude doesn't exist, watch home directory
   377→		watchDir = homeDir
   378→		watchingFor = ".claude"
   379→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory does not exist")
   380→		slog.Info("Claude directory not found, watching for creation of ~/.claude\n")
   381→	} else {
   382→		// .claude exists but projects doesn't, watch .claude
   383→		watchDir = claudeDir
   384→		watchingFor = "projects"
   385→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory exists, checking for projects")
   386→		slog.Info("Claude directory found, watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   387→	}
   388→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Will watch directory",
   389→		"watchDir", watchDir,
   390→		"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   391→
   392→	// Create watcher
   393→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   394→	if err != nil {
   395→		log.UserWarn("Failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   396→		slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to create file watcher", "error", err)
   397→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	// Add directory to watch
   401→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Adding directory to watcher", "directory", watchDir)
   402→	if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   403→		_ = watcher.Close() // Clean up on error; close errors less important than Add error
   404→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to watch directory %s: %v", watchDir, err)
   405→	}
   406→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started watching", "directory", watchDir)
   407→
   408→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   409→	go func() {
   410→		defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }() // Cleanup on exit; errors not recoverable
   411→
   412→		for {
   413→			select {
   414→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   415→				if !ok {
   416→					return
   417→				}
   418→
   419→				slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Received event",
   420→					"operation", event.Op.String(),
   421→					"path", event.Name)
   422→				// Check if this is the directory we're waiting for (case-insensitive)
   423→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), watchingFor) {
   424→					slog.Info("Detected creation", "path", event.Name)
   425→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Target directory created", "path", event.Name)
   426→
   427→					// Wait a second to let things settle
   428→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Waiting 1 second for things to settle")
   429→					time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
   430→
   431→					// Check what we should do next
   432→					if watchingFor == ".claude" {
   433→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude was created, checking if projects exists")
   434→						// We were watching for .claude, now check if projects exists
   435→						if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   436→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory does not exist, switching watcher")
   437→							// Need to watch for projects directory
   438→							_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; switching watch targets
   439→							watchDir = claudeDir
   440→							watchingFor = "projects"
   441→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Switching to watch",
   442→								"watchDir", watchDir,
   443→								"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   444→							if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   445→								log.UserWarn("Error switching to watch .claude directory: %v", err)
   446→								slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to switch watcher", "error", err)
   447→								return
   448→							}
   449→							slog.Info("Now watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   450→							continue
   451→						} else {
   452→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory already exists!")
   453→						}
   454→					}
   455→
   456→					// If we get here, projects directory should exist
   457→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Projects directory should now exist, cleaning up watcher")
   458→					// Remove the current watcher
   459→					_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; we're transitioning to project watcher
   460→
   461→					// Start the normal project watcher
   462→					slog.Info("Claude projects directory now exists, starting normal file watcher\n")
   463→					// Try to get the project directory again
   464→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Getting project directory name")
   465→					if projectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(""); err == nil {
   466→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Project directory will be", "directory", projectDir)
   467→						if err := startProjectWatcher(projectDir); err != nil {
   468→							log.UserWarn("Error starting project watcher: %v", err)
   469→							slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to start project watcher", "error", err)
   470→						} else {
   471→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started project watcher")
   472→						}
   473→					} else {
   474→						log.UserWarn("Error getting project directory after Claude setup: %v", err)
   475→						slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to get project directory", "error", err)
   476→					}
   477→					return
   478→				}
   479→
   480→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   481→				if !ok {
   482→					return
   483→				}
   484→				log.UserWarn("Watcher error: %v", err)
   485→				slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Watcher error", "error", err)
   486→			}
   487→		}
   488→	}()
   489→
   490→	return nil
   491→}
   492→
   493→// convertToAgentChatSession converts a Session to an AgentChatSession
   494→// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
   495→func convertToAgentChatSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
   496→	// Skip empty sessions (no records)
   497→	if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   498→		return nil
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	// Write debug files first (even for warmup-only sessions)
   502→	_ = make(map[int]int) // recordToFileNumber no longer needed
   503→	if debugRaw {
   504→		// Write debug files
   505→		writeDebugRawFiles(session)
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	// Filter warmup messages
   509→	filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
   510→
   511→	// Skip if no real messages remain after filtering warmup
   512→	if len(filteredRecords) == 0 {
   513→		slog.Debug("Skipping warmup-only session", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid)
   514→		return nil
   515→	}
   516→
   517→	// Create session with filtered records
   518→	filteredSession := Session{
   519→		SessionUuid: session.SessionUuid,
   520→		Records:     filteredRecords,
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	// Get timestamp from root record
   524→	rootRecord := filteredSession.Records[0]
   525→	timestamp, ok := rootRecord.Data["timestamp"].(string)
   526→	if !ok {
   527→		slog.Warn("convertToAgentChatSession: No timestamp found in root record")
   528→		return nil
   529→	}
   530→
   531→	// Get the slug
   532→	slug := FileSlugFromRootRecord(filteredSession)
   533→
   534→	// Generate SessionData from filtered session
   535→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(filteredSession, workspaceRoot)
   536→	if err != nil {
   537→		slog.Error("Failed to generate SessionData", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid, "error", err)
   538→		return nil
   539→	}
   540→
   541→	// Convert session records to JSONL format for raw data
   542→	// Note: Uses unfiltered session.Records (not filteredSession.Records) to preserve
   543→	// all records including warmup messages in the raw data for complete audit trail
   544→	var rawDataBuilder strings.Builder
   545→	for _, record := range session.Records {
   546→		jsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(record.Data)
   547→		rawDataBuilder.Write(jsonBytes)
   548→		rawDataBuilder.WriteString("\n")
   549→	}
   550→
   551→	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
   552→		SessionID:   session.SessionUuid,
   553→		CreatedAt:   timestamp, // ISO 8601 timestamp
   554→		Slug:        slug,
   555→		SessionData: sessionData,
   556→		RawData:     rawDataBuilder.String(),
   557→	}
   558→}
   559→
```

```text
    12→// GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir returns the path to the Claude Code projects directory
    13→func GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir() (string, error) {
    14→	// Get user's home directory
    15→	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
    16→	if err != nil {
    17→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
    18→	}
    19→
    20→	// Construct the path to .claude/projects
    21→	projectsDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects")
    22→
    23→	// Check if the directory exists
    24→	if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); err != nil {
    25→		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
    26→			return "", fmt.Errorf("claude projects directory not found at %s", projectsDir)
    27→		}
    28→		return "", fmt.Errorf("error checking Claude projects directory: %v", err)
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	return projectsDir, nil
    32→}
    33→
    34→// GetClaudeCodeProjectDir returns the Claude Code project directory for the given path.
    35→// If no path is provided (empty string), it uses the current working directory.
    36→// It resolves any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior, which uses the real path
    37→// when creating project directories. Claude Code requires a specific naming convention to map
    38→// working directories to project folders: each non-alphanumeric character is replaced with a dash
    39→// to create a unique, filesystem-safe identifier
    40→func GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    41→	// Use provided path or get current working directory
    42→	var cwd string
    43→	var err error
    44→	if projectPath == "" {
    45→		cwd, err = os.Getwd()
    46→		if err != nil {
    47→			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
    48→		}
    49→	} else {
    50→		cwd = projectPath
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// Resolve any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior
    54→	// Claude Code uses the real path when creating project directories, so
    55→	// if the user's working directory contains symlinks, we need to resolve
    56→	// them to find the correct project directory in ~/.claude/projects/
    57→	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
    58→	if err != nil {
    59→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve symlinks in working directory: %v", err)
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	// Log if the path was changed by symlink resolution
    63→	if realPath != cwd {
    64→		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Resolved symlinks in working directory",
    65→			"original", cwd,
    66→			"resolved", realPath)
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	// Convert path to project directory format using regex
    70→	// Replace anything that's not alphanumeric or dash with a dash (matching Claude Code's behavior)
    71→	// Example: "/Users/sean/My Projects(1)/app" becomes "-Users-sean-My-Projects-1--app"
    72→	reg := regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
    73→	projectDirName := reg.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
    74→
    75→	// Add leading dash if not already there
    76→	if !strings.HasPrefix(projectDirName, "-") {
    77→		projectDirName = "-" + projectDirName
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	// Log the transformation for debugging path issues
    81→	slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Transformed working directory to project name",
    82→		"cwd", cwd,
    83→		"projectDirName", projectDirName)
    84→
    85→	// Get Claude projects directory
    86→	projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get Claude projects directory: %v", err)
    89→	}
    90→
    91→	fullProjectPath := filepath.Join(projectsDir, projectDirName)
    92→	slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Constructed full project path",
    93→		"projectPath", fullProjectPath)
    94→
    95→	return fullProjectPath, nil
    96→}
    97→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-18 10:35:23Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-18 10:35:32Z)**_

Now I have a full picture. Here's what's happening:

**Root cause**: `setupDirectoryWatches` in `droidcli/watcher.go:88-100` uses `filepath.WalkDir` to walk ALL subdirectories under `~/.factory/sessions/` and registers a watcher on every one. It doesn't know anything about the current project at that point.

The `projectPath` filtering only happens *later* in `processSessionFile` (line 152) when events actually fire — by reading the JSONL file content via `sessionMentionsProject`. So it watches everything but only processes files for the relevant project.

**Contrast with `claudecode`**: The claude provider has `GetClaudeCodeProjectDir` which derives the exact project-specific subdirectory name from the project path (replacing non-alphanumeric chars with `-`), and `startProjectWatcher` only watches *that one directory*.

Looking at the factory session directory names in the log:
- Project `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/compositions/droid-1` → `~/.factory/sessions/-Users-sean-Source-SpecStory-compositions-droid-1`

Same naming convention — path slashes replaced with dashes. So droidcli *could* derive the exact project session directory the same way.

**Fix options**:

1. **Targeted watch (recommended)**: Add a `resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)` function in `droidcli/path_utils.go` (same regex as `GetClaudeCodeProjectDir`), then pass `projectPath` to `setupDirectoryWatches` so it only watches the one directory that belongs to the current project. If that directory doesn't exist yet, fall back to watching `sessionsDir` root for its creation.

2. **Filter during walk**: Keep `WalkDir` but skip subdirectories whose names don't match the expected project-derived dir name. Less clean, same result.

3. **Do nothing / suppress the log**: The behavior is functionally correct — wrong-project events are filtered before processing. But it wastes file descriptors and is confusing. Not recommended.

Option 1 is clearly the right fix and mirrors how the claude provider works. Want me to implement it?

```text
     1→package droidcli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"io/fs"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"strings"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
    13→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    14→)
    15→
    16→type watchState struct {
    17→	lastProcessed map[string]int64
    18→}
    19→
    20→func watchSessions(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    21→	if sessionCallback == nil {
    22→		return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
    23→	}
    24→
    25→	sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
    26→	if err != nil {
    27→		return err
    28→	}
    29→
    30→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
    31→	if err != nil {
    32→		return fmt.Errorf("droidcli: failed to create watcher: %w", err)
    33→	}
    34→	defer func() {
    35→		_ = watcher.Close()
    36→	}()
    37→
    38→	state := &watchState{lastProcessed: make(map[string]int64)}
    39→
    40→	// Initial scan to process existing sessions
    41→	if err := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); err != nil {
    42→		slog.Debug("droidcli: initial scan failed", "error", err)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	// Setup directory watching (handles non-existent dirs)
    46→	if err := setupDirectoryWatches(watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath); err != nil {
    47→		slog.Debug("droidcli: setup watches failed", "error", err)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	// Event loop
    51→	for {
    52→		select {
    53→		case <-ctx.Done():
    54→			return ctx.Err()
    55→		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
    56→			if !ok {
    57→				return nil
    58→			}
    59→			handleWatchEvent(event, watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
    60→		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
    61→			if !ok {
    62→				return nil
    63→			}
    64→			slog.Debug("droidcli: watcher error", "error", err)
    65→		}
    66→	}
    67→}
    68→
    69→// setupDirectoryWatches adds watches for the relevant session directories.
    70→// When projectPath is set, only the project's derived subdirectory is watched so
    71→// that unrelated projects' session directories are not monitored.
    72→// If the required directory doesn't exist yet, parent directories are watched so
    73→// creation can be detected.
    74→func setupDirectoryWatches(watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string, projectPath string) error {
    75→	// Check if sessions directory exists
    76→	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
    77→		// Watch parent (.factory) for sessions dir creation
    78→		parentDir := filepath.Dir(sessionsDir)
    79→		if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
    80→			// Watch home for .factory creation
    81→			homeDir := filepath.Dir(parentDir)
    82→			slog.Debug("droidcli: watching home for .factory creation", "path", homeDir)
    83→			return watcher.Add(homeDir)
    84→		}
    85→		slog.Debug("droidcli: watching .factory for sessions creation", "path", parentDir)
    86→		return watcher.Add(parentDir)
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	// When a project path is known, watch only that project's session subdirectory
    90→	// rather than every project's directory under sessionsDir.
    91→	if projectPath != "" {
    92→		projectSessionDir, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
    93→		if err != nil {
    94→			slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to resolve project session dir, falling back to all", "error", err)
    95→		} else if projectSessionDir != "" {
    96→			if _, err := os.Stat(projectSessionDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
    97→				// Project dir not created yet; watch sessionsDir so we see it when it appears.
    98→				slog.Debug("droidcli: watching sessions dir for project directory creation", "path", sessionsDir)
    99→				return watcher.Add(sessionsDir)
   100→			}
   101→			slog.Debug("droidcli: watching project session directory", "path", projectSessionDir)
   102→			return watcher.Add(projectSessionDir)
   103→		}
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	// No project filter (or resolution failed): watch sessionsDir and all subdirectories.
   107→	return filepath.WalkDir(sessionsDir, func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, err error) error {
   108→		if err != nil {
   109→			return nil
   110→		}
   111→		if d.IsDir() {
   112→			if watchErr := watcher.Add(path); watchErr != nil {
   113→				slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to watch directory", "path", path, "error", watchErr)
   114→			} else {
   115→				slog.Debug("droidcli: watching directory", "path", path)
   116→			}
   117→		}
   118→		return nil
   119→	})
   120→}
   121→
   122→// handleWatchEvent processes fsnotify events, adding watches to new directories
   123→// and processing changed JSONL files.
   124→func handleWatchEvent(event fsnotify.Event, watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
   125→	// Handle directory creation - add new watch
   126→	if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   127→		info, err := os.Stat(event.Name)
   128→		if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   129→			// When sessionsDir itself is created, set up watches properly.
   130→			if event.Name == sessionsDir {
   131→				if err := setupDirectoryWatches(watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath); err != nil {
   132→					slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to setup watches for new sessions dir", "error", err)
   133→				}
   134→				return
   135→			}
   136→			// For a new project subdirectory, only watch it if it belongs to the current project.
   137→			if isTargetProjectDir(event.Name, projectPath) {
   138→				if watchErr := watcher.Add(event.Name); watchErr == nil {
   139→					slog.Debug("droidcli: watching new directory", "path", event.Name)
   140→				}
   141→			}
   142→			return
   143→		}
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	// Only process .jsonl files
   147→	if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".jsonl") {
   148→		return
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	// Only process Create and Write events
   152→	if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
   153→		return
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	slog.Debug("droidcli: jsonl file event", "op", event.Op.String(), "file", event.Name)
   157→
   158→	// Process the specific file that changed
   159→	processSessionFile(event.Name, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
   160→}
   161→
   162→// processSessionFile processes a single JSONL file if it has been modified.
   163→func processSessionFile(filePath string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
   164→	info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		return
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	modTime := info.ModTime().UnixNano()
   170→	if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[filePath]; ok && last >= modTime {
   171→		return
   172→	}
   173→
   174→	if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(filePath, projectPath) {
   175→		return
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	session, err := parseFactorySession(filePath)
   179→	if err != nil {
   180→		slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session", "path", filePath, "error", err)
   181→		return
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
   185→	if chat == nil {
   186→		return
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	state.lastProcessed[filePath] = modTime
   190→	dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
   191→}
   192→
   193→// scanAndProcessSessions scans all session files and processes any that have been modified.
   194→func scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) error {
   195→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
   196→	if err != nil {
   197→		return err
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	for _, file := range files {
   201→		if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[file.Path]; ok && last >= file.ModTime {
   202→			continue
   203→		}
   204→		if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
   205→			continue
   206→		}
   207→		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
   208→		if err != nil {
   209→			slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
   210→			continue
   211→		}
   212→		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
   213→		if chat == nil {
   214→			continue
   215→		}
   216→		state.lastProcessed[file.Path] = file.ModTime
   217→		dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
   218→	}
   219→	return nil
   220→}
   221→
   222→func dispatchSession(sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   223→	if sessionCallback == nil || session == nil {
   224→		return
   225→	}
   226→	go func() {
   227→		defer func() {
   228→			if r := recover(); r != nil {
   229→				slog.Error("droidcli: session callback panicked", "panic", r)
   230→			}
   231→		}()
   232→		sessionCallback(session)
   233→	}()
   234→}
   235→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-18 10:43:00Z)**_

Clean. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**`path_utils.go`** — added three new functions:
- `projectPathToSessionDirName`: converts a project path to a factory session directory name using the same regex convention as Claude Code (non-alphanumeric chars → `-`).
- `resolveProjectSessionDir`: builds the full `~/.factory/sessions/<derived-name>` path for a given project. Returns `""` if `projectPath` is empty (meaning "watch all").
- `isTargetProjectDir`: used in event handling to decide whether a newly-created directory should be watched.

**`watcher.go`** — three call sites updated:
- `setupDirectoryWatches` now takes `projectPath`. When set, it watches only that project's session directory (or `sessionsDir` as a staging area if the project dir doesn't exist yet). When empty, falls back to the original walk-everything behaviour.
- `handleWatchEvent`'s directory-creation branch: passes `projectPath` to `setupDirectoryWatches`, and only adds a watch for new subdirectories that `isTargetProjectDir` approves.
- `watchSessions` passes `projectPath` through to `setupDirectoryWatches`.

The result: `specstory watch` in `watch-3` will now log a single `droidcli: watching project session directory` line for `~/.factory/sessions/-Users-sean-Source-SpecStory-compositions-watch-3` instead of one line for every project on the machine.

---

```bash
package main
import (

)
func main() {
	cwd, _ := os.Getwd()
	fmt.Println("Getwd:", cwd)

	fmt.Println("EvalSymlinks:", real, err)

	name := reg.ReplaceAllString(real, "-")
	if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") { name = "-" + name }

	fmt.Println("Actual dir name: -Users-sean-Source-SpecStory-compositions-watch-3")
}
```

The log only has startup entries — zero runtime events. This tells me the `Create` event for the new directory either never reached `handleWatchEvent`, or got silently swallowed. There's no logging for the "isTargetProjectDir returned false" case, and no logging at the top of `handleWatchEvent` for received events. I'm flying blind.

Two problems to fix:

1. **No visibility**: `handleWatchEvent` has no log when events are received or when `isTargetProjectDir` returns false
2. **Race condition**: even if we DO add the watch on the new directory, the JSONL file may have been created before we got there — we need an immediate scan after adding the watch

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-19 14:47:10Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-19 14:47:13Z)**_

Found it. Crystal clear:

```
path=/Users/sean/.factory/sessions/-Users-sean-Source-SpecStory-compositions-watch-4
project=/Users/sean/source/SpecStory/compositions/watch-4
```

**Case mismatch.** The Factory CLI records sessions under `Source` (uppercase S), but `os.Getwd()` returned `source` (lowercase s) — likely because `~/source` is navigated via a case-insensitive path. `filepath.EvalSymlinks` doesn't normalize case on macOS, it only resolves symlinks. So our derived directory name has `source` → doesn't match `Source` → rejected.

The `spi` package already has `GetCanonicalPath` which explicitly handles case normalization on macOS. That's what we need here.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-19 14:47:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/providers/droidcli/path_utils.go`

```text
     1→package droidcli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"io/fs"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→	"regexp"
     9→	"sort"
    10→	"strings"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// nonAlphanumericDash matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash,
    14→// mirroring the naming convention Factory CLI uses for session subdirectories.
    15→var nonAlphanumericDash = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
    16→
    17→// projectPathToSessionDirName converts a project path to the factory session directory name.
    18→// Factory CLI uses the same convention as Claude Code: non-alphanumeric characters (except
    19→// dashes) are replaced with dashes, and a leading dash is prepended.
    20→func projectPathToSessionDirName(projectPath string) string {
    21→	name := nonAlphanumericDash.ReplaceAllString(projectPath, "-")
    22→	if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
    23→		name = "-" + name
    24→	}
    25→	return name
    26→}
    27→
    28→// resolveProjectSessionDir returns the factory sessions subdirectory for a specific project path.
    29→// Returns "" if projectPath is empty (caller should watch all sessions).
    30→func resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    31→	if strings.TrimSpace(projectPath) == "" {
    32→		return "", nil
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	// Resolve symlinks to match Factory CLI's real-path behaviour when it creates directories.
    36→	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(projectPath)
    37→	if err != nil {
    38→		// Path may not exist yet; proceed with the given path.
    39→		realPath = projectPath
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
    43→	if err != nil {
    44→		return "", err
    45→	}
    46→
    47→	return filepath.Join(sessionsDir, projectPathToSessionDirName(realPath)), nil
    48→}
    49→
    50→// isTargetProjectDir returns true if path is a session directory that should be watched.
    51→// When projectPath is set, only the project's derived directory qualifies; otherwise
    52→// all directories qualify (the no-filter / sync-all case).
    53→func isTargetProjectDir(path, projectPath string) bool {
    54→	if projectPath == "" {
    55→		return true
    56→	}
    57→	expected, err := resolveProjectSessionDir(projectPath)
    58→	if err != nil || expected == "" {
    59→		// Can't derive expected dir; allow everything to avoid missing sessions.
    60→		return true
    61→	}
    62→	return path == expected
    63→}
    64→
    65→const (
    66→	factoryRootDir     = ".factory"
    67→	factorySessionsDir = "sessions"
    68→)
    69→
    70→type sessionFile struct {
    71→	Path     string
    72→	ModTime  int64
    73→	FileName string
    74→}
    75→
    76→func resolveSessionsDir() (string, error) {
    77→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
    78→	if err != nil {
    79→		return "", fmt.Errorf("droidcli: cannot resolve home dir: %w", err)
    80→	}
    81→	dir := filepath.Join(home, factoryRootDir, factorySessionsDir)
    82→	return dir, nil
    83→}
    84→
    85→func listSessionFiles() ([]sessionFile, error) {
    86→	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
    87→	if err != nil {
    88→		return nil, err
    89→	}
    90→	files := make([]sessionFile, 0, 64)
    91→	walker := func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, walkErr error) error {
    92→		if walkErr != nil {
    93→			return nil
    94→		}
    95→		if d.IsDir() {
    96→			return nil
    97→		}
    98→		if filepath.Ext(d.Name()) != ".jsonl" {
    99→			return nil
   100→		}
   101→		info, err := d.Info()
   102→		if err != nil {
   103→			return nil
   104→		}
   105→		files = append(files, sessionFile{
   106→			Path:     path,
   107→			ModTime:  info.ModTime().UnixNano(),
   108→			FileName: d.Name(),
   109→		})
   110→		return nil
   111→	}
   112→	if err := filepath.WalkDir(dir, walker); err != nil {
   113→		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
   114→			return []sessionFile{}, nil
   115→		}
   116→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("droidcli: unable to read sessions dir: %w", err)
   117→	}
   118→	sort.Slice(files, func(i, j int) bool {
   119→		return files[i].ModTime > files[j].ModTime
   120→	})
   121→	return files, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→func findSessionFileByID(sessionID string) (string, error) {
   125→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
   126→	if err != nil {
   127→		return "", err
   128→	}
   129→	for _, file := range files {
   130→		candidate := file.FileName
   131→		if candidate == sessionID || trimExt(candidate) == sessionID {
   132→			return file.Path, nil
   133→		}
   134→	}
   135→	return "", nil
   136→}
   137→
   138→func trimExt(name string) string {
   139→	ext := filepath.Ext(name)
   140→	if ext == "" {
   141→		return name
   142→	}
   143→	return name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
   144→}
   145→
   146→// settingsPathFromSession returns the path to the .settings.json file
   147→// that corresponds to a session JSONL file.
   148→// Example: /path/to/session.jsonl -> /path/to/session.settings.json
   149→func settingsPathFromSession(sessionPath string) string {
   150→	dir := filepath.Dir(sessionPath)
   151→	base := filepath.Base(sessionPath)
   152→	ext := filepath.Ext(base)
   153→	nameWithoutExt := base[:len(base)-len(ext)]
   154→	return filepath.Join(dir, nameWithoutExt+".settings.json")
   155→}
   156→
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-19 14:47:42Z)**_

The root cause was `filepath.EvalSymlinks` only resolves symlinks — it doesn't normalize case. On macOS's case-insensitive filesystem, navigating via `~/source` (lowercase) leaves `os.Getwd()` returning `source`, but Factory CLI stores the directory under `Source` (uppercase, the real on-disk name). `spi.GetCanonicalPath` walks the path component by component, reading actual directory entries to correct the case, so it will produce `Source` regardless of how the user typed it.

Ready for another test run with `watch-5`.

---

Test GetCanonicalPath with the actual lowercase path

```bash
cat > /tmp/check_canonical.go << 'EOF'
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"unicode"
	"golang.org/x/text/runes"
	"golang.org/x/text/transform"
	"golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm"
	_ "golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm"
)

func appendRemainingParts(result string, parts []string, startIdx int) string {
	for j := startIdx; j < len(parts); j++ {
		if parts[j] != "" {
			result = filepath.Join(result, parts[j])
		}
	}
	return result
}

func GetCanonicalPath(p string) (string, error) {
	p = filepath.Clean(p)
	if !filepath.IsAbs(p) {
		var err error
		p, err = filepath.Abs(p)
		if err != nil {
			return "", err
		}
	}
	realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(p)
	if err == nil && realPath != p {
		p = realPath
	}
	parts := strings.Split(p, string(os.PathSeparator))
	result := string(os.PathSeparator)
	for i := 1; i < len(parts); i++ {
		if parts[i] == "" {
			continue
		}
		f, err := os.Open(result)
		if err != nil {
			return appendRemainingParts(result, parts, i), nil
		}
		names, err := f.Readdirnames(-1)
		_ = f.Close()
		if err != nil {
			return "", err
		}
		found := false
		for _, name := range names {
			if strings.EqualFold(name, parts[i]) {
				result = filepath.Join(result, name)
				found = true
				break
			}
		}
		if !found {
			result = appendRemainingParts(result, parts, i)
			break
		}
	}
	return result, nil
}

func main() {
	_ = runes.Remove(runes.In(unicode.Mn))
	_ = transform.String
	_ = norm.NFD

	p := "/Users/sean/source/SpecStory/compositions/watch-6"
	canonical, err := GetCanonicalPath(p)
	fmt.Printf("Input:     %s\nCanonical: %s\nError: %v\n", p, canonical, err)
}
EOF
cd /tmp && go run check_canonical.go 2>&1
```
